View towards the northern façade of the court of the Louvre showing the pavilion Turgot, the pavilion Richelieu, and the pavilion Colbert.  In the right foreground is the pavilion Mollien.  In the center of the court is a garden containing trees and a statue of a man on a horse (possibly Napoleon).  Note the close-coupled columns, carved stone detail piled upon carved stone detail, pediments within pediments, massive pitched and curved roofs and promiscuous statuary on each of the pavilions.
